content.full_text:
Incurring Expenses on Behalf of Another - in-kind contribution and accrued expenses

If a person uses his personal funds to incur expenses on behalf of a candidate, he generally will have made an in-kind contribution to the candidate. However, if the person is close to the campaign and if the candidate has agreed to pay back the person who has incurred expenses on his behalf in a short period of time such as two weeks, an in-kind contribution may not have been made. This result is to avoid a reportable in-kind contribution in the situation where a person makes expenditures on behalf of a candidate on or just before the closing date for a campaign statement and then is reimbursed by the candidate a few days later.

Whether or not the expenditure constitutes an in-kind contribution, it is reportable by the campaign as an accrued expense.
